message:
✨ Add CLUSTER_NAME env var for explicit in-cluster naming

Add clusterName Helm value (→ CLUSTER_NAME env var) so admins can set
a friendly name like "vllm-d" or "pok-prod-001" instead of the
auto-detected name. CLUSTER_NAME takes priority over OpenShift
Infrastructure detection, which takes priority over "in-cluster".

Set clusterName=vllm-d for vllm-d and clusterName=pok-prod-001 for
pok-prod in the CI workflow.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

files.patch:
@@ -136,6 +136,7 @@ jobs:
             --set googleDrive.existingSecret=kc-secrets \
             --set route.enabled=true \
             --set route.host=kc.apps.fmaas-vllm-d.fmaas.res.ibm.com \
+            --set clusterName=vllm-d \
             --set-string enabledDashboards="dashboard\,clusters\,gpu-reservations\,llm-d-benchmarks\,ai-ml\,arcade" \
             --wait \
             --timeout 5m
@@ -192,6 +193,7 @@ jobs:
             --set googleDrive.existingSecret=kc-kubestellar-console \
             --set route.enabled=true \
             --set route.host=kc-kubestellar-console-kubestellar-console.apps.pokprod001.ete14.res.ibm.com \
+            --set clusterName=pok-prod-001 \
             --set-string enabledDashboards="dashboard\,clusters\,gpu-reservations\,llm-d-benchmarks\,ai-ml\,arcade" \
             --wait \
             --timeout 5m

@@ -144,6 +144,9 @@ kubestellar:
 # Example: "gpu-reservations,llm-d-benchmarks"
 enabledDashboards: ""
 
+# Override the auto-detected in-cluster name (e.g. "vllm-d", "pok-prod")
+clusterName: ""
+
 nodeSelector: {}
 
 tolerations: []
